---
title: "Inference Task"
author: "Daniel Tsai"
date: "2025-10-08"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(lubridate)
library(janitor)
library(sandwich)
library(lmtest)
library(broom)
library(grf)
library(glue)
library(showtext)
library(ggplot2)
showtext_auto()
set.seed(5166)

# Configure knitr options for reproducible output
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  echo = TRUE,
  fig.width = 8,
  fig.height = 6
)
```

```{r data-loading}
# Load member and sales data
members <- read_csv("members-group.csv")
sales <- read_csv("sales-2024.csv")
```

## Compute purchase frequency and product preference
```{r calculate-frequency}
# Coerce quantity to numeric and drop non-positive
sales_clean <- sales %>%
  mutate(quantity = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(quantity))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(quantity) & quantity > 0)

# Total cups in 2024 (strictly > 0 per member)
cups_2024 <- sales_clean %>%
  mutate(member_id = as.character(member_id)) %>%
  group_by(member_id) %>%
  summarise(BaselineFreq = sum(quantity, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(BaselineFreq > 0)

# Tea vs Coffee preference
category_pref <- sales_clean %>%
  mutate(
    member_id = as.character(member_id),
    is_tea = category %in% c("調味茶", "奶茶"),
    is_coffee = category %in% c("純咖啡", "調味咖啡")
  ) %>%
  group_by(member_id) %>%
  summarise(
    tea_cups = sum(quantity[is_tea], na.rm = TRUE),
    coffee_cups = sum(quantity[is_coffee], na.rm = TRUE),
    total_cups = sum(quantity, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    tea_ratio = tea_cups / total_cups,
    coffee_ratio = coffee_cups / total_cups,
    prefer_tea = tea_ratio > 0.5,
    prefer_coffee = coffee_ratio > tea_ratio & !prefer_tea
  ) %>%
  filter(total_cups > 0)

members_b <- members %>%
  mutate(member_id = as.character(member_id)) %>% 
  left_join(cups_2024, by = "member_id") %>%
  left_join(category_pref, by = "member_id") %>%
  mutate(
    BaselineFreq = replace_na(BaselineFreq, 0),
    total_cups = replace_na(total_cups, 0),
    prefer_tea = replace_na(prefer_tea, FALSE),
    prefer_coffee = replace_na(prefer_coffee, FALSE),
    # Card ordering: Diamond, Gold, Silver
    card_level = factor(card_level, levels = c("鑽石", "金", "銀")),
    # English display labels
    card_level_en = forcats::fct_recode(card_level,
      Diamond = "鑽石", Gold = "金", Silver = "銀"
    ),
    gender_en = dplyr::recode(as.character(gender), "男" = "Male", "女" = "Female")
  ) %>%
  # Drop members with zero baseline cups and zero total cups
  filter(BaselineFreq > 0, total_cups > 0)

# Sanity check (should be 0)
cat("Min BaselineFreq:", min(members_b$BaselineFreq, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("# with BaselineFreq == 0:", sum(members_b$BaselineFreq == 0, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Min total_cups:", min(members_b$total_cups, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("# with total_cups == 0:", sum(members_b$total_cups == 0, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")

members_b %>%
  summarise(
    mean_cups = mean(BaselineFreq),
    sd_cups   = sd(BaselineFreq),
    median    = median(BaselineFreq),
    max       = max(BaselineFreq)
  ) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Total Cups Bought per Member (2024)", digits = 2) %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
```

## Convert birthday to age
```{r convert-age}
# Convert birthday to age
members_b <- members_b %>%
  mutate(
    birthday = ymd(birthday),
    Age = floor(interval(birthday, ymd("2024-12-31")) / years(1))
  )
```

## 1-1 Balance check

### Numeric variables
```{r balance-numeric}
# Balance check for numeric variables
numeric_balance <- members_b %>%
  group_by(CouponType) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_age = mean(Age, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_age   = sd(Age, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_freq = mean(BaselineFreq, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_freq   = sd(BaselineFreq, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

numeric_balance %>%
  kbl(caption = "Balance Check – Numeric Variables (Age, BaselineFreq)", digits = 3) %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
```

```{r balance-plots}
# Age distribution
p1 <- ggplot(members_b, aes(x = Age, fill = CouponType)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Age Distribution by Coupon Type",
       x = "Age", y = "Density")

print(p1)

# BaselineFreq distribution (strictly positive only)
p2 <- ggplot(filter(members_b, BaselineFreq > 0), aes(x = BaselineFreq, fill = CouponType)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Purchase Frequency by Coupon Type",
       x = "Total Cups (2024)", y = "Density")

print(p2)
```

### Categorical variables
```{r balance-categorical}
# Gender composition by Coupon Type (English)
if("gender_en" %in% names(members_b)) {
  p3 <- ggplot(members_b, aes(x = CouponType, fill = gender_en)) +
    geom_bar(position = "fill") +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    labs(
      title = "Gender Composition by Coupon Type",
      x = "Coupon Type",
      y = "Share within Group (%)",
      fill = "Gender"
    )
  print(p3)
}

if("card_level_en" %in% names(members_b)) {
  members_b <- members_b %>% mutate(card_level_en = forcats::fct_relevel(card_level_en, "Diamond", "Gold", "Silver"))
  p4 <- ggplot(members_b, aes(x = CouponType, fill = card_level_en)) +
    geom_bar(position = "fill") +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Diamond" = "#7b1fa2", "Gold" = "#fbc02d", "Silver" = "#90a4ae")) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    labs(
      title = "Membership Tier Composition by Coupon Type",
      x = "Coupon Type",
      y = "Share within Group (%)",
      fill = "Tier"
    )
  print(p4)
}
```

### Comprehensive balance table
```{r comprehensive-balance-table}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)
library(purrr)
library(kableExtra)
library(scales)

df <- members_b

# Define variable types for balance checks
cont_vars <- c("Age", "BaselineFreq")
cat_vars  <- c("card_level_en", "gender_en")
group_var <- "CouponType"

# Ensure group variable is character type
df <- df %>% mutate(CouponType = as.character(.data[[group_var]]))

# ============================================================================
# Continuous variables: compute means, SDs, and perform t-tests
# ============================================================================
cont_summary <- map_dfr(cont_vars, function(var) {
  stats <- df %>%
    group_by(CouponType) %>%
    summarise(
      mean = mean(.data[[var]], na.rm = TRUE),
      sd   = sd(.data[[var]], na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  # Extract statistics for R and U groups
  m_R  <- stats$mean[stats$CouponType == "R"]
  sd_R <- stats$sd[stats$CouponType == "R"]
  m_U  <- stats$mean[stats$CouponType == "U"]
  sd_U <- stats$sd[stats$CouponType == "U"]

  # Perform two-sample t-test
  ttest <- tryCatch(
    t.test(df[[var]] ~ df$CouponType),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  tibble(
    Variable = paste0("avg_", var),
    Mean_R   = round(m_R, 3),
    SD_R     = round(sd_R, 3),
    Mean_U   = round(m_U, 3),
    SD_U     = round(sd_U, 3),
    Diff     = round(m_R - m_U, 3),
    P_value  = if (!is.null(ttest)) round(ttest$p.value, 4) else NA_real_
  )
})

# ============================================================================
# Categorical variables: compute proportions and perform proportion tests
# ============================================================================
cat_summary <- map_dfr(cat_vars, function(v) {
  if (!v %in% names(df)) return(tibble())
  
  prop_df <- df %>%
    count(CouponType, !!sym(v), name = "n") %>%
    group_by(CouponType) %>%
    mutate(N_group = sum(n), prop = n / N_group) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    rename(level = !!sym(v))

  all_levels <- sort(unique(prop_df$level))

  prop_full <- tidyr::expand_grid(CouponType = c("R","U"), level = all_levels) %>%
    left_join(prop_df, by = c("CouponType","level")) %>%
    mutate(
      n = replace_na(n, 0),
      N_group = ave(n, CouponType, FUN = function(x) rep(sum(x), length(x))),
      prop = replace_na(prop, 0)
    )

  by_level <- prop_full %>%
    arrange(level, CouponType) %>%
    group_split(level)

  map_dfr(by_level, function(g) {
    r <- g %>% filter(CouponType == "R")
    u <- g %>% filter(CouponType == "U")

    pv <- tryCatch(
      prop.test(x = c(r$n, u$n), n = c(r$N_group, u$N_group))$p.value,
      error = function(e) NA_real_
    )

    se_R <- sqrt(r$prop * (1 - r$prop) / r$N_group)
    se_U <- sqrt(u$prop * (1 - u$prop) / u$N_group)

    tibble(
      Variable = paste0("prop_", v, "=", as.character(r$level)),
      Mean_R   = r$prop,
      SD_R     = se_R,
      Mean_U   = u$prop,
      SD_U     = se_U,
      Diff     = r$prop - u$prop,
      P_value  = pv
    )
  })
})

# ============================================================================
# Combine and format results for display
# ============================================================================
summary_table <- bind_rows(cont_summary, cat_summary) %>%
  mutate(
    P_value = format.pval(as.numeric(P_value), digits = 3, eps = 0.001)
  )

# Identify proportion rows for formatting
is_prop <- grepl("^prop_", summary_table$Variable)

summary_table_display <- summary_table %>%
  mutate(
    Mean_R  = ifelse(is_prop, percent(Mean_R, accuracy = 0.1), sprintf("%.3f", Mean_R)),
    SD_R    = ifelse(is_prop, percent(SD_R,   accuracy = 0.1), sprintf("%.3f", SD_R)),
    Mean_U  = ifelse(is_prop, percent(Mean_U, accuracy = 0.1), sprintf("%.3f", Mean_U)),
    SD_U    = ifelse(is_prop, percent(SD_U,   accuracy = 0.1), sprintf("%.3f", SD_U)),
    Diff    = ifelse(is_prop, percent(Diff,   accuracy = 0.1), sprintf("%.3f", Diff))
  )

# Apply human-readable variable names
name_map <- c(
  "avg_Age" = "Avg. Age",
  "avg_BaselineFreq" = "Avg. Total Cups (2024)",
  "prop_card_level_en=Diamond" = "% Diamond",
  "prop_card_level_en=Gold" = "% Gold",
  "prop_card_level_en=Silver" = "% Silver",
  "prop_gender_en=Female" = "% Female",
  "prop_gender_en=Male" = "% Male"
)
summary_table_display$Variable <- recode(summary_table_display$Variable, !!!name_map)

# Render balance table
kable(
  summary_table_display,
  format = "html",
  col.names = c("Variable",
                "Mean (R)", "SE (R)",
                "Mean (U)", "SE (U)",
                "Diff (R - U)", "p-value"),
  align = c("l","c","c","c","c","c"),
  booktabs = TRUE,
  caption = "Summary Statistics by Coupon Type (R vs U)"
) %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover")) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "R (Restricted)" = 2, "U (Universal)" = 2, "Comparison" = 2))
```

# Question 2

```{r load-2025-data}
# Load 2025 sales data for treatment effect analysis
sales_2025 <- read_csv("sales-2025.csv")
```

## Preprocessing

### Define treatment and control
```{r define-treatment}
# Create binary treatment indicator (R = 1, U = 0)
members_b <- members_b %>%
  mutate(
    CouponType = as.character(CouponType),
    treat = case_when(
      CouponType %in% c("R") ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0
    )
  )
```

## 2-1: ATE of R vs U on redemption

```{r q2-1-ate-analysis}
# =============================================================================
# Average Treatment Effect on Redemption (without time fixed effects)
# =============================================================================

# Step 1: Identify members who redeemed birthday coupons
redeem_2025 <- sales_2025 %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  mutate(
    member_id          = as.character(member_id),
    total_price        = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(total_price)),
    is_birthday_coupon = suppressWarnings(as.integer(is_birthday_coupon))
  ) %>%
  filter(
    payment_method == "Coupon",
    coalesce(total_price, 0) == 0,
    is_birthday_coupon == 1L
  ) %>%
  distinct(member_id) %>%
  mutate(redeemed = 1L)

# Step 2: Build analysis dataset with treatment and covariates
df_q2 <- members_b %>%
  mutate(
    member_id = as.character(member_id),
    CouponType = as.character(CouponType),
    treat = if_else(toupper(CouponType) == "R", 1, 0),
    Age = as.numeric(Age),
    BaselineFreq = as.numeric(BaselineFreq)
  ) %>%
  left_join(redeem_2025, by = "member_id") %>%
  mutate(redeemed = replace_na(redeemed, 0L),
         gender = factor(gender),
         card_level = factor(card_level))

# Step 2.5: Sanity check - raw redemption rates by group
redeem_rate <- df_q2 %>% group_by(CouponType) %>% summarise(rate = mean(redeemed), n = n(), .groups = "drop")
cat("[Q2-1 Sanity Check] Redemption rate by group:\n"); print(redeem_rate)

# =============================================================================
# Estimation Method 1: Linear Regression with Robust Standard Errors
# =============================================================================
get_robust_treat_q21 <- function(data) {
  m <- lm(redeemed ~ treat + Age + BaselineFreq + gender + card_level, data = data)
  r <- coeftest(m, vcov = vcovHC(m, type = "HC1"))
  # Robustly locate treatment coefficient
  rn <- rownames(r)
  tr_idx <- which(rn == "treat" | rn == "treat1" | rn == "treatTRUE")
  if (length(tr_idx) == 0) stop("treat coefficient not found in model")
  c(estimate = r[tr_idx, "Estimate"], se = r[tr_idx, "Std. Error"], p = r[tr_idx, "Pr(>|t|)"])
}

lm_vec <- get_robust_treat_q21(df_q2)
lm_ate <- unname(lm_vec[["estimate"]])
lm_se  <- unname(lm_vec[["se"]])
lm_pval<- unname(lm_vec[["p"]])

# =============================================================================
# Estimation Method 2: Causal Forest (Machine Learning Approach)
# =============================================================================
mm <- model.matrix(~ Age + BaselineFreq + gender + card_level, data = df_q2)
X <- mm[, -1, drop = FALSE]
W <- as.numeric(df_q2$treat)
Y <- as.numeric(df_q2$redeemed)

# Remove incomplete cases and constant columns
cc <- is.finite(rowSums(X)) & is.finite(W) & is.finite(Y)
Xc <- X[cc, , drop = FALSE]
Wc <- W[cc]
Yc <- Y[cc]
keep <- apply(Xc, 2, function(col) { v <- stats::var(col, na.rm = TRUE); is.finite(v) && !is.na(v) && v > 0 })
Xc <- Xc[, keep, drop = FALSE]

if (length(unique(Wc)) < 2 || stats::var(Yc) <= 0 || ncol(Xc) == 0) {
  cf_ate <- NA_real_; cf_se <- NA_real_; cf_pval <- NA_real_
} else {
  set.seed(5166)
  cf <- causal_forest(Xc, Yc, Wc, num.trees = 2000)
  ate <- tryCatch(average_treatment_effect(cf), error = function(e) c(NA_real_, NA_real_))
  cf_ate <- ate[1]; cf_se <- ate[2]
  if (!is.finite(cf_ate) || !is.finite(cf_se) || cf_se == 0) {
    tau <- tryCatch(as.numeric(predict(cf)$predictions), error = function(e) rep(NA_real_, length(Wc)))
    cf_ate <- mean(tau, na.rm = TRUE)
    cf_se  <- sd(tau, na.rm = TRUE)/sqrt(sum(is.finite(tau)))
  }
  cf_pval <- if (is.finite(cf_se) && cf_se > 0) 2*(1 - pnorm(abs(cf_ate/cf_se))) else NA_real_
}

# =============================================================================
# Display results: ATE comparison table
# =============================================================================
ate_table <- tibble(
  Method = c("Linear Regression (Robust SE)", "Causal Forest"),
  ATE = c(lm_ate, cf_ate),
  SE = c(lm_se, cf_se),
  `95% CI Lower` = c(lm_ate - 1.96*lm_se, cf_ate - 1.96*cf_se),
  `95% CI Upper` = c(lm_ate + 1.96*lm_se, cf_ate + 1.96*cf_se),
  `p-value` = c(lm_pval, cf_pval)
) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    ATE = sprintf("%.4f", .data$ATE),
    SE = sprintf("%.4f", .data$SE),
    `95% CI Lower` = sprintf("%.4f", .data[["95% CI Lower"]]),
    `95% CI Upper` = sprintf("%.4f", .data[["95% CI Upper"]]),
    `p-value` = format.pval(.data[["p-value"]], digits = 3, eps = 0.001)
  )

kable(
  ate_table,
  format = "html",
  caption = "2-1: Average Treatment Effect of Restricted vs Universal Coupon on Redemption",
  align = c("l", "c", "c", "c", "c", "c")
) %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover")) %>%
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE)

# =============================================================================
# Causal Forest: Variable Importance Analysis
# =============================================================================
if (exists("cf") && inherits(cf, "causal_forest") && exists("Xc")) {
  vi <- tryCatch(variable_importance(cf), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (!is.null(vi)) {
    fn <- tryCatch(colnames(Xc), error = function(e) NULL)
    if (!is.null(fn) && length(fn) == length(vi)) names(vi) <- fn

    vi_tbl <- tibble(
      Feature = if (!is.null(names(vi))) names(vi) else paste0("X", seq_along(vi)),
      Importance = as.numeric(vi)
    ) %>%
      dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(.data$Importance)) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(Share = .data$Importance / sum(.data$Importance))

    kable(
      vi_tbl %>% dplyr::mutate(
        Importance = sprintf("%.3f", .data$Importance),
        Share = scales::percent(.data$Share, accuracy = 0.1)
      ),
      format = "html",
      caption = "2-1: Feature Importance in Causal Forest Model",
      col.names = c("Feature", "Importance", "Share of Total"),
      align = c("l", "c", "c")
    ) %>%
      kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"))

    # Prepare data for plotting to avoid NSE warnings
    vi_tbl_plot <- vi_tbl %>% dplyr::mutate(Feature_ord = factor(.data$Feature, levels = rev(.data$Feature)))

    suppressWarnings({
      ggplot(vi_tbl_plot, aes(x = .data$Feature_ord, y = .data$Importance)) +
        geom_col(fill = "#4a90e2") +
        coord_flip() +
        theme_minimal() +
        labs(title = "Feature Importance for Treatment Effect Heterogeneity", 
             subtitle = "Higher values indicate stronger influence on treatment effect variation",
             x = "Feature", y = "Importance Score") %>%
        print()
    })
  }
}

cat("\n=== Key Findings ===\n")
cat(sprintf("Linear Regression ATE: %.4f (SE = %.4f)\n", lm_ate, lm_se))
cat(sprintf("Causal Forest ATE: %.4f (SE = %.4f)\n", cf_ate, cf_se))
cat(sprintf("\nInterpretation: Restricted coupons change redemption probability by %.2f percentage points compared to Universal coupons.\n",
            abs(lm_ate) * 100))

```  
# Subgroup Analysis: Heterogeneous Treatment Effects
```{r q2-subgroup-helpers}
# =============================================================================
# Helper Function: Subgroup HTE Analysis (LM uses interaction term)
# =============================================================================
build_subgroup_table <- function(df_in, outcome, title) {
  require(tidyverse)
  require(sandwich)
  require(lmtest)
  require(grf)
  require(kableExtra)

  # setting S
  baseline_median <- stats::median(df_in$BaselineFreq, na.rm = TRUE)
  df <- df_in %>%
    mutate(
      .row_id     = dplyr::row_number(),
      age_under45 = .data$Age < 45,
      freq_high   = .data$BaselineFreq >= baseline_median
    )

  # ---- Linear： model with interaction term
 lm_interact <- function(df_full, dsub, ctrls = c("Age","BaselineFreq","gender","card_level")) {
  S_ids <- dsub$.row_id
  d_model <- df_full %>% mutate(S = as.integer(.row_id %in% S_ids))
  keep_ctrls <- ctrls[vapply(ctrls, function(v) dplyr::n_distinct(d_model[[v]]) > 1, logical(1))]
  fml <- as.formula(paste(outcome, "~", paste(c("treat","S","treat:S", keep_ctrls), collapse = " + ")))

  # Helper to pick coefficient name
  pick_name <- function(cands, pool) {
    cand <- intersect(cands, pool)
    if (length(cand)) cand[1] else NA_character_
  }

  out <- tryCatch({
    m   <- lm(fml, data = d_model)
    # robust vcov
    V   <- sandwich::vcovHC(m, type = "HC1")
    b   <- coef(m)
    bn  <- names(b)

    nm_treat <- pick_name(c("treat","treat1","treatTRUE"), bn)
    nm_int   <- pick_name(c("treat:S","S:treat","treat1:S","treatTRUE:S","treat:S1","S1:treat"), bn)

    b_treat <- if (!is.na(nm_treat)) unname(b[[nm_treat]]) else NA_real_
    b_int   <- if (!is.na(nm_int))   unname(b[[nm_int]])   else NA_real_

    # CATE for subgroup S=1 : tau + gamma
    cate_S1 <- b_treat + b_int
    # robust SE via delta method: Var(treat)+Var(int)+2Cov(treat,int)
    se_S1 <- tryCatch({
      if (any(is.na(c(nm_treat, nm_int)))) NA_real_ else {
        sqrt(V[nm_treat, nm_treat] + V[nm_int, nm_int] + 2*V[nm_treat, nm_int])
      }
    }, error = function(e) NA_real_)

  
    cate_S0 <- b_treat
    se_S0 <- tryCatch({
      if (is.na(nm_treat)) NA_real_ else sqrt(V[nm_treat, nm_treat])
    }, error = function(e) NA_real_)

  
    z_S1 <- if (is.finite(cate_S1) && is.finite(se_S1) && se_S1 > 0) cate_S1 / se_S1 else NA_real_
    p_S1 <- if (is.finite(z_S1)) 2*(1 - pnorm(abs(z_S1))) else NA_real_

    list(cate_S1 = cate_S1, se_S1 = se_S1, p_S1 = p_S1,
         cate_S0 = cate_S0, se_S0 = se_S0)
  }, error = function(e) {
    list(cate_S1 = NA_real_, se_S1 = NA_real_, p_S1 = NA_real_,
         cate_S0 = NA_real_, se_S0 = NA_real_)
  })

  out
}


  # ---- Causal forest
  cf_treat <- function(d) {
    mm <- model.matrix(~ Age + BaselineFreq + gender + card_level, data = d)
    X <- mm[, -1, drop = FALSE]
    W <- as.numeric(d$treat)
    Y <- as.numeric(d[[outcome]])
    cc <- is.finite(rowSums(X)) & is.finite(W) & is.finite(Y)
    Xc <- X[cc, , drop = FALSE]; Wc <- W[cc]; Yc <- Y[cc]
    if (length(Wc) == 0) return(list(te = NA_real_, se = NA_real_, n_cf = 0))
    keep <- apply(Xc, 2, function(col) { v <- stats::var(col, na.rm = TRUE); is.finite(v) && !is.na(v) && v > 0 })
    if (!any(keep)) return(list(te = NA_real_, se = NA_real_, n_cf = length(Wc)))
    Xc <- Xc[, keep, drop = FALSE]
    if (length(unique(Wc)) < 2 || stats::var(Yc) <= 0) return(list(te = NA_real_, se = NA_real_, n_cf = length(Wc)))
    set.seed(5166)
    out <- tryCatch({
      cf <- grf::causal_forest(Xc, Yc, Wc, num.trees = 2000)
      ate <- tryCatch(grf::average_treatment_effect(cf), error = function(e) c(NA_real_, NA_real_))
      te <- ate[1]; se <- ate[2]
      if (!is.finite(se) || se == 0) {
        tau <- tryCatch(as.numeric(predict(cf)$predictions), error = function(e) rep(NA_real_, length(Wc)))
        te <- mean(tau, na.rm = TRUE)
        se <- stats::sd(tau, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(is.finite(tau)))
      }
      list(te = te, se = se, n_cf = length(Wc))
    }, error = function(e) list(te = NA_real_, se = NA_real_, n_cf = length(Wc)))
    out
  }

  # ---- Prepare rows
  rows <- list()
  add_row <- function(category, label, dsub) {
    n <- nrow(dsub)
    if (n == 0) return(NULL)
    lmres <- lm_interact(df, dsub) 
    cfres <- cf_treat(dsub) 
    rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <<- tibble(
  Category = category,
  Subgroup = label,
  N = n,
  TE_LM = lmres$cate_S1,  
  SE_LM = lmres$se_S1,
  P_LM  = lmres$p_S1,
  TE_CF = cfres$te,
  SE_CF = cfres$se,
  `N (CF)` = cfres$n_cf
)

  }

  # ---- Define subgroups and collect results
  add_row("Age Group", "Under 45",      df %>% dplyr::filter(.data$age_under45))
  add_row("Age Group", "45 and above",  df %>% dplyr::filter(!.data$age_under45))

  add_row("Product Preference", "Tea",   df %>% dplyr::filter(.data$prefer_tea == TRUE))
  add_row("Product Preference", "Coffee",df %>% dplyr::filter(.data$prefer_coffee == TRUE))

  map_cn_en <- c("鑽石" = "Diamond", "金" = "Gold", "銀" = "Silver")
  for (cn in names(map_cn_en)) {
    add_row("Card Level", map_cn_en[[cn]], df %>% dplyr::filter(as.character(.data$card_level) == cn))
  }

  add_row("Purchase Frequency", "Above median", df %>% dplyr::filter(.data$freq_high))
  add_row("Purchase Frequency", "Below median", df %>% dplyr::filter(!.data$freq_high))

  out <- dplyr::bind_rows(rows)

  # ---- Display table 
  out_disp <- out %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      TE_LM = ifelse(is.finite(.data$TE_LM), sprintf("%.4f", .data$TE_LM), "NA"),
      SE_LM = ifelse(is.finite(.data$SE_LM), sprintf("%.4f", .data$SE_LM), "NA"),
      P_LM  = ifelse(is.finite(.data$P_LM),  format.pval(.data$P_LM, digits = 3, eps = 0.001), "NA"),
      TE_CF = ifelse(is.finite(.data$TE_CF), sprintf("%.4f", .data$TE_CF), "NA"),
      SE_CF = ifelse(is.finite(.data$SE_CF), sprintf("%.4f", .data$SE_CF), "NA")
    )

  kable(
    out_disp,
    format = "html",
    col.names = c("Category","Subgroup","Sample Size",
                  "Effect ","Std. Error","p-value",
                  "Effect ","Std. Error","CF Sample"),
    caption = title,
    align = c("l","l","r","r","r","c","r","r","r")
  ) %>%
    add_header_above(c(" " = 2, " " = 1,
                       "Linear Model (Interaction: treat×S)" = 3,
                       "Causal Forest" = 3)) %>%
    kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover")) %>%
    print()

  invisible(out)
}

```

```{r q2-1-subgroup-tables}
# Analyze heterogeneous treatment effects across predefined subgroups
build_subgroup_table(df_q2, outcome = "redeemed", title = "2-1: Heterogeneous Treatment Effects on Redemption by Subgroup")
```



## 2-2: Post-1-month outcomes (Spend, Visits, Cups) — ATE and HTE

```{r q2-2-hte-helpers}
# =============================================================================
# Helper Functions for Q2-2: HTE estimation on post-period outcomes
# =============================================================================

# Linear model with full interaction terms
fit_lm_hte <- function(outcome, df) {
  f <- stats::as.formula(paste0(
    outcome,
    " ~ treat + Age + BaselineFreq + gender + card_level + age_under45 + tea50 + coffee_pref + freq_high"
  ))
  m <- lm(f, data = df)
  r <- lmtest::coeftest(m, vcov = sandwich::vcovHC(m, type = "HC1"))
  rn <- rownames(r)
  tr_idx <- which(rn %in% c("treat", "treat1", "treatTRUE"))
  if (length(tr_idx) == 0) stop("treat coefficient not found in model for ", outcome)
  list(
    method = "Linear Regression (Robust SE)",
    ate = unname(r[tr_idx, "Estimate"]),
    se  = unname(r[tr_idx, "Std. Error"]),
    p   = unname(r[tr_idx, "Pr(>|t|)"])
  )
}

# Causal forest with expanded feature set
fit_cf_hte <- function(outcome, df) {
  mm <- model.matrix(~ Age + BaselineFreq + gender + card_level + age_under45 + tea50 + coffee_pref + freq_high, data = df)
  X <- mm[, -1, drop = FALSE]
  W <- as.numeric(df$treat)
  Y <- as.numeric(df[[outcome]])

  cc <- is.finite(rowSums(X)) & is.finite(W) & is.finite(Y)
  Xc <- X[cc, , drop = FALSE]
  Wc <- W[cc]
  Yc <- Y[cc]
  keep <- apply(Xc, 2, function(col) { v <- stats::var(col, na.rm = TRUE); is.finite(v) && !is.na(v) && v > 0 })
  Xc <- Xc[, keep, drop = FALSE]

  if (length(unique(Wc)) < 2 || stats::var(Yc) <= 0 || ncol(Xc) == 0) {
    return(list(method = "Causal Forest", ate = NA_real_, se = NA_real_, p = NA_real_, forest = NULL, feature_names = colnames(Xc)))
  }

  set.seed(5166)
  cf <- grf::causal_forest(Xc, Yc, Wc, num.trees = 2000)
  ate <- tryCatch(grf::average_treatment_effect(cf), error = function(e) c(NA_real_, NA_real_))
  cf_ate <- ate[1]; cf_se <- ate[2]
  if (!is.finite(cf_ate) || !is.finite(cf_se) || cf_se == 0) {
    tau <- tryCatch(as.numeric(predict(cf)$predictions), error = function(e) rep(NA_real_, length(Wc)))
    cf_ate <- mean(tau, na.rm = TRUE)
    cf_se  <- stats::sd(tau, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(is.finite(tau)))
  }
  cf_pval <- if (is.finite(cf_se) && cf_se > 0) 2 * (1 - pnorm(abs(cf_ate / cf_se))) else NA_real_

  list(method = "Causal Forest", ate = cf_ate, se = cf_se, p = cf_pval, forest = cf, feature_names = colnames(Xc))
}

# Render ATE comparison table
render_hte_table <- function(lm_res, cf_res, title) {
  tab <- tibble(
    Method = c("Linear Regression (Robust SE)", "Causal Forest"),
    ATE = c(lm_res$ate, cf_res$ate),
    SE = c(lm_res$se, cf_res$se),
    `95% CI Lower` = c(lm_res$ate - 1.96 * lm_res$se, cf_res$ate - 1.96 * cf_res$se),
    `95% CI Upper` = c(lm_res$ate + 1.96 * lm_res$se, cf_res$ate + 1.96 * cf_res$se),
    `p-value` = c(lm_res$p, cf_res$p)
  ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      ATE = sprintf("%.4f", .data$ATE),
      SE = sprintf("%.4f", .data$SE),
      `95% CI Lower` = sprintf("%.4f", .data[["95% CI Lower"]]),
      `95% CI Upper` = sprintf("%.4f", .data[["95% CI Upper"]]),
      `p-value` = format.pval(.data[["p-value"]], digits = 3, eps = 0.001)
    )

  kable(
    tab,
    format = "html",
    caption = title,
    align = c("l", "c", "c", "c", "c", "c")
  ) %>%
    kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover")) %>%
    column_spec(1, bold = TRUE) %>%
    print()
}

# Render feature importance from causal forest
render_cf_importance <- function(cf_res, title_prefix) {
  if (is.null(cf_res$forest)) return(invisible(NULL))
  vi <- tryCatch(grf::variable_importance(cf_res$forest), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(vi)) return(invisible(NULL))
  if (!is.null(cf_res$feature_names) && length(cf_res$feature_names) == length(vi)) names(vi) <- cf_res$feature_names

  vi_tbl <- tibble(
    Feature = if (!is.null(names(vi))) names(vi) else paste0("X", seq_along(vi)),
    Importance = as.numeric(vi)
  ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(.data$Importance)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(Share = .data$Importance / sum(.data$Importance))

  kable(
    vi_tbl %>% dplyr::mutate(
      Importance = sprintf("%.3f", .data$Importance),
      Share = scales::percent(.data$Share, accuracy = 0.1)
    ),
    format = "html",
    caption = paste0(title_prefix, ": Feature Importance for Treatment Effect Variation"),
    col.names = c("Feature", "Importance Score", "Share of Total"),
    align = c("l", "c", "c")
  ) %>%
    kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover")) %>%
    print()

  vi_tbl_plot <- vi_tbl %>% dplyr::mutate(Feature_ord = factor(.data$Feature, levels = rev(.data$Feature)))

  suppressWarnings({
    ggplot(vi_tbl_plot, aes(x = .data$Feature_ord, y = .data$Importance)) +
      geom_col(fill = "#4a90e2") +
      coord_flip() +
      theme_minimal() +
      labs(title = paste0(title_prefix, ": Feature Importance Distribution"), 
           subtitle = "Higher scores indicate stronger predictive power for treatment heterogeneity",
           x = "Feature", y = "Importance Score") %>%
      print()
  })
}
```

```{r q2-2-post-outcomes}
# =============================================================================
# Step 0: Calculate post-period outcomes (cups, spending, visits)
# =============================================================================
post_paid <- sales_2025 %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  mutate(
    member_id   = as.character(member_id),
    total_price = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(total_price)),
    quantity    = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(quantity))
  ) %>%
  filter(
    is_post_window == 1,
    coalesce(total_price, 0) > 0,
    coalesce(quantity, 0) > 0
  ) %>%
  group_by(member_id) %>%
  summarise(
    cups_post   = sum(coalesce(quantity, 0), na.rm = TRUE),
    spend_post  = sum(coalesce(total_price, 0), na.rm = TRUE),
    visits_post = n_distinct(transaction_id),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Merge post-period outcomes with main analysis dataset
df_q2 <- df_q2 %>%
  left_join(post_paid, by = "member_id") %>%
  mutate(
    cups_post   = replace_na(cups_post, 0),
    spend_post  = replace_na(spend_post, 0),
    visits_post = replace_na(visits_post, 0)
  )

# =============================================================================
# Build enhanced analysis dataset with interaction terms
# =============================================================================
baseline_median <- median(df_q2$BaselineFreq, na.rm = TRUE)

df_ht2 <- df_q2 %>%
  mutate(
    age_under45  = as.integer(Age < 45),
    tea50        = as.integer(prefer_tea),
    coffee_pref  = as.integer(prefer_coffee),
    freq_high    = as.integer(BaselineFreq >= baseline_median),
    card_level   = factor(card_level)
  )

# ...existing code...
# =============================================================================
# Build enhanced analysis dataset with interaction terms
# =============================================================================
baseline_median <- median(df_q2$BaselineFreq, na.rm = TRUE)

df_ht2 <- df_q2 %>%
  mutate(
    age_under45  = as.integer(Age < 45),
    tea50        = as.integer(prefer_tea),
    coffee_pref  = as.integer(prefer_coffee),
    freq_high    = as.integer(BaselineFreq >= baseline_median),
    card_level   = factor(card_level)
  )
if ("銀" %in% levels(df_ht2$card_level)) df_ht2$card_level <- stats::relevel(df_ht2$card_level, ref = "銀")
stopifnot(all(c("cups_post","spend_post","visits_post") %in% names(df_ht2)))

# =============================================================================
# Outcome 1: Post-period spending
# =============================================================================
lm_spend  <- fit_lm_hte("spend_post", df_ht2)
cf_spend  <- fit_cf_hte("spend_post", df_ht2)
render_hte_table(lm_spend, cf_spend, "Treatment Effect on Post-Period Total Spending")
render_cf_importance(cf_spend, "Post-Period Spending")

# =============================================================================
# Outcome 2: Post-period visit frequency
# =============================================================================
lm_visits <- fit_lm_hte("visits_post", df_ht2)
cf_visits <- fit_cf_hte("visits_post", df_ht2)
render_hte_table(lm_visits, cf_visits, "Treatment Effect on Post-Period Visit Frequency")
render_cf_importance(cf_visits, "Post-Period Visits")

# =============================================================================
# Outcome 3: Post-period cups purchased
# =============================================================================
lm_cups   <- fit_lm_hte("cups_post", df_ht2)
cf_cups   <- fit_cf_hte("cups_post", df_ht2)
render_hte_table(lm_cups, cf_cups, "Treatment Effect on Post-Period Cups Purchased")
render_cf_importance(cf_cups, "Post-Period Cups")

# =============================================================================
# Subgroup analysis for all three post-period outcomes
# =============================================================================
build_subgroup_table(df_ht2, outcome = "spend_post",  title = "2-2: HTE on Post-Period Spending by Subgroup")
build_subgroup_table(df_ht2, outcome = "visits_post", title = "2-2: HTE on Post-Period Visits by Subgroup")
build_subgroup_table(df_ht2, outcome = "cups_post",   title = "2-2: HTE on Post-Period Cups by Subgroup")
```
